title: Learn compound-life windows from data instead of fixed constants
summary: Replace or augment hard-coded tyre-life assumptions with robust train-only quantile priors by compound.
rationale: Fixed compound expected-life constants and finish-feasibility margins are a plausible source of signal, but repeated reuse of fixed thresholds suggests that a learned version is underexplored. Train-only compound-life quantiles may capture race, year, or compound variation better than hard-coded windows.
implementation_hint: Extend step-65 preprocessing by estimating smoothed train-only compound tyre-life quantiles by Compound, such as Q60, Q75, and Q90. Add current-tyre and next-stop finish margins relative to those learned windows. Optionally smooth by Year and Race if sample sizes are sufficient. Keep the original fixed-window features in the first run as fallback features.
expected_effect: Medium potential ROC AUC improvement, especially in borderline situations where fixed constants are too coarse. It may improve worst-fold AUC and ranking near final-stop feasibility thresholds.
risk: Do not use PitNextLap. If PitStop is used as an observed stint endpoint, verify that it exists in both train and test and is safe at prediction time. Any event-derived statistic must be computed fold-safely. Using test-distribution-derived priors would add transductive risk and should be avoided in the first experiment.
